message:
fix(core): support mcp_agent_mail underscore alias in config detection and upgrade interner to RwLock

intern.rs: Replace the global Mutex with a RwLock using a double-checked
locking pattern. The fast path now acquires only a read lock for
already-interned strings, eliminating write-lock contention on the
critical request path. The write lock is only taken on first encounter
of a new string value, with a re-check under write to prevent
double-insertion races. intern_count() also switches to a read lock
since it only needs shared access.

mcp_config.rs: Introduce TARGET_SERVER_ALIASES containing both
"mcp-agent-mail" (canonical) and "mcp_agent_mail" (legacy underscore
variant). update_target_entry() now searches for either alias when
locating the existing server entry, canonicalizes the key to the
hyphenated form on write, and removes stale underscore aliases via
remove_noncanonical_target_aliases(). insert_server_entry_text() uses
find_target_server_key() to detect either alias as already-present,
preventing duplicate entries when a user's config has the underscore
variant. Two new tests cover both the noop-when-underscore-exists and
the update-and-canonicalize paths.

setup.rs: merge_mcp_server() now explicitly removes the opposite alias
(hyphen vs underscore) when merging the mcp-agent-mail entry, so a
single canonical entry is always left. check_config_file() broadens its
search to include the "mcp_servers" container key and both server name
variants, and returns (true, false) when the server is found but the URL
does not match (previously returned (false, false)). Four new tests
validate underscore alias detection, mcp_servers container recognition,
and alias deduplication during merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
